note: Schema diff (section 5.1). Each case: current + target schema (plain dicts), and the operations diff() must produce in order. Increment 1: table/column create/drop/rename/alter in the safe emit order; dependency ordering (5.4) and ambiguity (5.3) follow with FK/index ops.

cases.expect_sql: CREATE TABLE users (id uuid PRIMARY KEY, email text)

err.message: CHECK on 't' can be neither natively enforced on 'turso' nor compiled: x @@ 1

cases.expect_sql: CREATE TABLE products (price integer NOT NULL CHECK (price > 0))
ALTER TABLE users RENAME COLUMN mail TO email
ALTER TABLE tasks ADD CONSTRAINT fk_tasks_owner FOREIGN KEY (owner) REFERENCES users (id)
CREATE UNIQUE INDEX users_email ON users (email)

expect_contains: INSERT INTO _hp_tmp_users (id) SELECT id FROM users

expect_executed_contains: INSERT INTO _hp_tmp_users (id) SELECT id FROM users

cases.expect_sql: SELECT id, email FROM users WHERE status = :status
SELECT id FROM users JOIN orders ON orders.user_id = users.id WHERE status = :status AND age = :age ORDER BY name ASC, created DESC LIMIT :limit OFFSET :offset
UPDATE users SET status = :set_status WHERE id = :id
SELECT id, email FROM users WHERE status = :status

query.sql: SELECT id FROM users WHERE id = :id
SELECT id, name FROM users WHERE id = :id
SELECT id FROM users WHERE id = :id
SELECT count(*) FROM users WHERE id = :id

writes.sql: UPDATE users SET status = :set_status WHERE id = :id

expected.sql: UPDATE users SET name = :set_name WHERE id = :id

ok.sql: UPDATE users SET name = :set_name WHERE id = :id

err.message: manifest carries neither db_id nor tenant_id

expected.sql: UPDATE _hp_array_posts_tags SET value = :set_value WHERE owner_id = :owner_id AND ordinal = :ordinal
DELETE FROM _hp_array_posts_tags WHERE owner_id = :owner_id AND ordinal = :ordinal
UPDATE _hp_array_posts_tags SET ordinal = ordinal - 1 WHERE owner_id = :owner_id AND ordinal > :removed
DELETE FROM _hp_map_posts_meta WHERE owner_id = :owner_id AND key = :key
INSERT INTO _hp_closure_nodes (ancestor, descendant, depth) SELECT ancestor, :node, depth + 1 FROM _hp_closure_nodes WHERE descendant = :parent UNION ALL SELECT :node, :node, 0
DELETE FROM _hp_closure_nodes WHERE descendant IN (SELECT descendant FROM _hp_closure_nodes WHERE ancestor = :node)

DELETE FROM _hp_closure_nodes WHERE descendant IN (SELECT descendant FROM _hp_closure_nodes WHERE ancestor = :node) AND ancestor NOT IN (SELECT descendant FROM _hp_closure_nodes WHERE ancestor = :node)

INSERT INTO _hp_closure_nodes (ancestor, descendant, depth) SELECT super.ancestor, sub.descendant, super.depth + sub.depth + 1 FROM _hp_closure_nodes super CROSS JOIN _hp_closure_nodes sub WHERE super.descendant = :new_parent AND sub.ancestor = :node
SELECT * FROM t WHERE id > :after ORDER BY id LIMIT :limit

value_oracle_exempt.reason: Its dependency map is keyed by non-string keys, which cannot round-trip a JSON value contract.
Assembles a DiffResult whose dependency map is keyed by non-string keys; not a portable value.
Async schema-apply boundary: executes the diff against a live connection; its effect is the schema change, verified by its own conformance.
Async I/O boundary: runs a query against a live connection.
Async I/O boundary: runs a query against a live connection.
Async I/O boundary: runs a query against a live connection.
Async I/O boundary: runs a query against a live connection.
Async I/O boundary: runs writes in a transaction against a live connection.
Takes detailed column specifications; covered by the to_sql / apply reconstruction paths.
Async I/O boundary: runs a query against a live connection and emits through an injected emit. Verified by its own conformance probe.
Async I/O boundary: creates a connection through the injected connect and threads the pool cache. Verified by its own conformance probe against SQLite.
Async I/O boundary: closes idle on_demand connections through the injected close and threads the pool cache. Verified by its own conformance probe.
Async I/O boundary: connects and applies the target schema for each ephemeral database at startup. Verified by its own conformance probe against SQLite.
Async I/O boundary: emits a pool lifecycle event through the injected emit, swallowing failures. Verified by its own conformance probe.
Async I/O boundary: persists each pending write to the backend through the injected execute. Verified by its own conformance probe against SQLite.
I/O boundary: writes the queue's JSONL form to its file. Verified by its own conformance probe.
I/O boundary: reads the queue from its JSONL file. Verified by its own conformance probe.
Async I/O boundary: drains the queue through the injected execute, emits and raises on stall. Verified by its own conformance probe.
I/O boundary: appends a write and persists the queue to its JSONL file. Verified by its own conformance probe.
Async I/O boundary: drains the queue in the background, retrying with backoff through the injected clock and sleep. Verified by its own conformance probe.
Returns a (result, pool) tuple, which cannot round-trip a JSON value contract; verified through the connection-pool law probe.
An I/O boundary: acquires a connection and emits a pool event through the injected emit; verified against real SQLite in the law probe.

An I/O boundary: establishes each connection through connect_with_retry, closing any already opened if a later one fails, and emits a created event; verified against real SQLite in the connection-pool law probe.
An I/O boundary: closes each connection through the injected close and emits a closed event; verified against real SQLite in the law probe.
An I/O boundary: connects through the injected connect, catching driver errors to retry or fault, and emits pool events; verified against real SQLite in the connect-retry law probe.
An I/O boundary: reads the live database catalog through the injected connection; verified against real SQLite in the migration-workflow law probe.
An I/O orchestrator: composes inspect, diff, and apply against a live database; verified against real SQLite in the migration-workflow law probe.
An I/O boundary: reads batches from the source and writes rows to the destination; verified between two real SQLite databases in the cutover law probe.
An I/O boundary: dual-writes a query to the source and destination connections; verified between two real SQLite databases in the cutover law probe.
